title: 屏幕共享 工具 for Support: 2026年 对比 Guide
description: 对比 顶级 screen sharing 工具 for 客户 支持 in 2026年. Review 功能, ease of use, and 价格 for TeamViewer, Zoom, and specialized 支持 platfo...获取专业建议，优化你的客服流程！
content:
Understanding Screen Sharing for Customer Support

Screen sharing tools have revolutionized customer support by enabling visual communication that transcends traditional phone and email interactions. In 2026, support teams leverage screen sharing to observe customer issues directly, provide guided solutions, and reduce miscommunication that often occurs with verbal descriptions alone.

The best screen sharing tools for support differ from general video conferencing platforms by offering specialized features like remote control capabilities, annotation tools, and integration with support workflows. These purpose-built solutions help technicians diagnose issues faster and guide customers through complex resolution steps with visual assistance.

Screen Sharing Tools Comparison Table

Our comparison evaluates screen sharing platforms specifically designed for or adapted to customer support scenarios. Key criteria include ease of customer connection, support-specific features, pricing, and overall reliability.

ToolConnection MethodRemote ControlAnnotationMonthly Cost
TeamViewer QuickSupportQuick codeFull controlYes$49
Zoom Screen ShareMeeting linkRequest onlyBasic$40
ConnectWise ControlBrowser/codeFull controlAdvanced$27
AnyDeskAddress codeFull controlYes$29
Splashtop SOSSession codeFull controlYes$17
SurflyBrowser linkCobrowsingAdvanced$59

Essential Features for Support Screen Sharing

Distinguishing between video conferencing screen sharing and purpose-built support tools requires understanding the features that specifically benefit support workflows. Support teams need capabilities beyond simple screen viewing.

  • Remote Control Access: True support tools allow technicians to control customer screens directly, enabling hands-on troubleshooting rather than verbal guidance alone. This capability dramatically reduces resolution time for technical issues.
  • Annotation and Highlighting: Drawing tools help technicians direct customer attention to specific interface elements, buttons, or settings. Visual guidance proves more effective than verbal instructions for navigating complex applications.
  • Easy Customer Connection: Customers should connect without installing software or navigating complex setup processes. Browser-based connections, simple access codes, and one-click invitations minimize friction at the start of support interactions.
  • Session Recording: Recording capabilities support quality assurance, training purposes, and compliance documentation. Recorded sessions help identify common issues and improve support processes over time.
  • Mobile Device Support: Modern customers frequently need assistance on smartphones and tablets. Screen sharing tools must support mobile platforms with appropriate viewing and control capabilities.

TeamViewer QuickSupport: Professional Standard

TeamViewer QuickSupport provides a streamlined customer-side module that requires no installation and connects instantly via simple access codes. Customers download a lightweight executable, receive a unique ID, and technicians connect immediately for support sessions.

The platform's full remote control capabilities allow technicians to diagnose and resolve issues hands-on, while annotation tools help guide customers when direct intervention isn't necessary or desired. TeamViewer's cross-platform support ensures technicians can assist customers regardless of operating system.

Security implementation includes end-to-end encryption and optional two-factor authentication for technician accounts. Session recording captures both screen activity and chat communication for comprehensive documentation. TeamViewer's pricing reflects its premium feature set, starting at $49 monthly for professional use.

Zoom for Support: Video-Conferencing Adapted

Zoom's screen sharing capabilities serve dual purposes for support teams already using the platform for meetings. While not purpose-built for support, Zoom offers sufficient features for many support scenarios, particularly those requiring verbal explanation alongside visual demonstration.

Technicians can request remote control of shared screens, though this requires customer approval for each action. Zoom's annotation tools include basic drawing and highlighting capabilities adequate for guiding customers through straightforward processes.

Zoom excels when support sessions involve multiple participants or require collaboration among support team members. The platform's familiarity reduces customer resistance, as many users already have Zoom installed and understand the interface. Pricing at $40 monthly provides good value for organizations leveraging Zoom for multiple communication needs.

ConnectWise Control: Customizable Support Solution

ConnectWise Control offers exceptional customization options for support teams wanting branded, tailored customer experiences. The platform supports browser-based connections eliminating software installation requirements, though downloadable agents provide enhanced functionality.

Advanced annotation tools exceed most competitors, with drawing, highlighting, text overlay, and screenshot capture during sessions. Technicians can save annotated images for customer reference or training documentation, adding value beyond immediate session support.

ConnectWise Control's toolbox feature allows running predefined scripts and deploying tools during sessions, automating common diagnostic and repair tasks. This automation capability significantly reduces time spent on repetitive troubleshooting procedures. At $27 monthly, ConnectWise Control delivers exceptional value for feature-rich support operations.

AnyDesk: Performance-Oriented Sharing

AnyDesk's proprietary video codec delivers superior frame rates and responsiveness, making screen sharing smooth even on bandwidth-constrained connections. This performance advantage proves critical when supporting customers in regions with limited internet infrastructure or during high-demand periods.

The platform's lightweight design minimizes customer system impact during sessions. Quick connection via address codes requires minimal customer effort, reducing session setup friction. Remote control capabilities meet professional support needs with appropriate security measures.

AnyDesk's pricing at $29 monthly positions it competitively between budget and premium options. The platform suits support teams prioritizing session performance and reliability over extensive advanced features.

Splashtop SOS: Budget-Friendly Option

Splashtop SOS provides robust screen sharing and remote control capabilities at the lowest monthly cost among professional solutions. Starting at $17 monthly, the platform delivers essential support features without premium pricing.

Session codes simplify customer connection, while the technician console provides standard remote control, annotation, and file transfer capabilities. Splashtop's mobile support is particularly strong, allowing technicians to view and control iOS and Android devices effectively.

While lacking some advanced features like ConnectWise's toolbox or TeamViewer's enterprise management, Splashtop SOS meets core support requirements reliably. The platform suits small support teams and organizations seeking cost-effective screen sharing solutions.

Surfly: Cobrowsing Specialization

Surfly specializes in cobrowsing rather than traditional screen sharing, enabling technicians to navigate alongside customers within web applications. This approach provides guidance without full remote control, addressing security concerns while maintaining effective support.

Browser-based connection requires no customer software installation. Technicians see exactly what customers see in web applications, can highlight elements, and guide navigation through complex web interfaces. Surfly's approach proves particularly valuable for e-commerce, SaaS, and banking support scenarios.

Pricing at $59 monthly reflects the specialized cobrowsing focus. Organizations with web-centric support needs find Surfly's targeted capabilities more appropriate than general remote desktop tools.

Best Practices for Screen Sharing Support

Effective screen sharing support requires skills beyond traditional phone or chat support. Technicians must learn to communicate while viewing customer screens, provide visual guidance, and balance observation with intervention.

  • Ask Before Acting: Request permission before taking remote control actions, even when customers have technically granted access. This courtesy builds trust and helps customers understand what technicians are doing.
  • Use Annotation First: Attempt visual guidance through highlighting and drawing before resorting to direct control. Annotation helps customers learn and reduces dependency on ongoing support for similar issues.
  • Document Sessions: Record sessions for training review and quality assurance. Screenshots of problem states and resolution steps provide valuable reference materials for future support interactions.
  • Minimize Session Duration: Efficient screen sharing reduces customer inconvenience. Prepare diagnostic approaches before connecting and resolve issues promptly to maintain positive customer experience.

Choosing the Right Screen Sharing Tool

Platform selection depends on support team size, budget, and specific use cases. Small teams with budget constraints benefit from Splashtop SOS's essential features at minimal cost. Professional support operations requiring comprehensive capabilities find TeamViewer's premium feature set appropriate.

Organizations already using Zoom for communication should evaluate whether Zoom's screen sharing meets support needs before investing in dedicated tools. Support teams with web-focused scenarios should consider Surfly's cobrowsing specialization. ConnectWise Control offers the best balance of features and value for most mid-sized support operations.

faq.question: 如何 es cobrowsing 不同 从 traditional screen sharing?
faq.answer: Cobrowsing allows technicians to navigate alongside 客户s within web applications without full remote control. This approach provides visual guidance while maintaining 客户 autonomy, addressing security concerns common in financial and healthcare support scenarios.

faq.question: 什么 annotation features help most in support sessions?
faq.answer: Essential annotation features include freehand drawing, arrow pointing, text overlay, highlighting boxes, and screenshot capture. These 工具 direct 客户 attention to specific elements and create visual reference materials for future use.

faq.question: Can screen sharing work effectively on mobile devices?
faq.answer: Modern screen sharing 工具 support iOS and Android devices through specialized viewing and control modes. Mobile screen sharing typically shows device screens on technician consoles, with varying degrees of remote control capability depending on platform and operating system restrictions.
